lede: A look at March 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Gerardus Mercator and Rosa Luxemburg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: ZX81
events.desc: The ZX81, a pioneering British home computer, is launched by Sinclair Research and would go on to sell over 11⁄2 million units around the world.

events.title: BOAC Flight 911
events.desc: BOAC Flight 911, a Boeing 707 aircraft, breaks apart in mid-air due to clear-air turbulence and crashes into Mount Fuji, Japan, killing all 124 people on board.

events.title: Joseph Stalin
events.desc: Joseph Stalin, the longest serving leader of the Soviet Union, dies at his Volynskoe dacha in Moscow after suffering a cerebral hemorrhage four days earlier.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese forces capture Batavia, capital of Dutch East Indies, which is left undefended after the withdrawal of the KNIL garrison and Australian Blackforce battalion to Buitenzorg and Bandung.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler's Nazi Party receives 43.9% at the Reichstag elections, which allows the Nazis to later pass the Enabling Act and establish a dictatorship.

events.title: Roberto Cofresí
events.desc: Roberto Cofresí, one of the last successful Caribbean pirates, is defeated in combat and captured by authorities.

events.title: First Anglo-Burmese War
events.desc: First Anglo-Burmese War: The British officially declare war on Burma.

events.title: Peninsular War
events.desc: Peninsular War: A French force under the command of Marshal Victor is routed while trying to prevent an Anglo-Spanish-Portuguese army from lifting the Siege of Cádiz in the Battle of Barrosa.

events.title: Nicolaus Copernicus
events.desc: Nicolaus Copernicus's book On the Revolutions of the Heavenly Spheres is added to the Index of Forbidden Books 73 years after it was first published.

events.title: Livonian Order
events.desc: The Livonian Order is defeated in the Battle of Aizkraukle by the Grand Duchy of Lithuania.

births.name: Zhou Enlai
births.line: Chinese politician, 1st Premier of the People's Republic of China

births.name: Bernard Arnault
births.line: French businessman, philanthropist, and art collector

births.name: Louis I of Hungary
births.line: King of Hungary and Croatia from 1342 to 1382

deaths.text: Hugo Chávez, Venezuelan colonel and politician, President of Venezuela
Joseph Stalin, Soviet dictator and politician of Georgian descent, 2nd leader of the Soviet Union
Antonio da Correggio, Italian painter and educator

observances.text: Christian feast day: John Joseph of the Cross
Christian feast day: Theophilus, bishop of Caesarea
